message.content:
CONTEXT. POLARIS just banked a HONEST RACE result: 0.4447 overall (Comp 0.457, Insight 0.429,
Instruction 0.459, Readability 0.431), faithfulness PASS, general, reproduced. It beats claude-3-7-sonnet
(0.4218) but is ~0.035 below the ~0.48 frontier — and closing that gap requires RACE length-tuning the
operator FORBADE. So we bank RACE and now prove the axis where POLARIS is structurally SUPERIOR:
DeepTRACE (citation faithfulness — does every cited claim actually get supported by its source).

Worktree: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box, HEAD d8547a2, clean).
POLARIS report to score: outputs/step3_control/report.md (the 0.4447 report) and its packed form
third_party/deep_research_bench/data/test_data/raw_data/polaris_step3_control.jsonl.
DeepTRACE tooling: /workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py,
deeptrace_judge_preflight.py, config/benchmark/deeptrace_judge_lock.yaml (signed, judge kimi-k2.6,
self_rescore policy). Competitors: /workspace/POLARIS/competitors/*.md (ChatGPT/Gemini scoped+unscoped).
Source the key: set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a (never print it).

HONESTY (non-negotiable): report REAL measured DeepTRACE numbers. A KNOWN prior finding: competitor
reports may NOT be scoreable on DeepTRACE because they lack parseable [N] citations + fetched source
content (ChatGPT uses opaque UI tokens, Gemini bare URLs). If that holds, that is itself the finding —
POLARIS is claim-by-claim AUDITABLE and verified; the competitors are largely UN-AUDITABLE. Do NOT
fake a head-to-head number. Never tune the judge. If we can only score ourselves, say so and frame it
honestly (auditability gap), not as a fabricated win.


YOU ARE FABLE. Give the honest verdict on the FAITHFULNESS axis.
CLEANUP: {
  "removed": [
    "multi_section_generator.py: _SYNTH_QUANT_ENV constant + _synthesis_quant_directive_enabled() default-OFF flag gate (was ~lines 915-924)",
    "multi_section_generator.py: _SYNTH_ROLE_SIGNAL vocabulary + _is_cross_study_synthesis_section() structural role detector (~926-944)",
    "multi_section_generator.py: _NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE + _row_carries_number() + _synthesis_enrich_cap() enrichment helpers (~947-971)",
    "multi_section_generator.py: _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK directive prompt text, incl. d8547a2's hygiene de-overfit edit (~974-998)",
    "multi_section_generator.py: _call_section() directive-injection call site (was ~4282-4293)",
    "multi_section_generator.py: generate_multi_section_report() ev_ids synthesis-enrichment call site (was ~10787-10834)",
    "tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_synthesis_insight_directive.py: entire file (8 synthesis-insight lever tests) deleted via git rm"
  ],
  "tests_pass": "82/82 passed, 0 failed (python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/outline/ -q). This is the full outline suite with the 8 removed lever tests gone; ~90 before removal minus the 8 lever tests == 82, consistent with the '8 synthesis-insight tests' spec.",
  "control_unchanged": true,
  "commit": "fce91efe069eb071cfd76be0af9d6bbde59d07bc",
  "notes": "DELETE-BY-HAND (no git revert): as warned, d8547a2's hygiene edit lives inside _SYNTHESIS_QUANT_BLOCK, so `git revert 31f488e` conflicts; I removed the lever by hand instead. Diff is a PURE deletion: `git diff --stat` = 1 file, 163 deletions, 0 additions (matches 31f488e's 163-line insertion into this file).\n\nBYTE-IDENTITY PROOF: `git diff 31f488e^ -- src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py` is EMPTY (0 lines). The working file is now byte-identical to f1ecf14, the pre-lever parent. Because the lever was default-OFF, the banked 0.4447 report (directive OFF) never executed this code path, so the compose CONTROL path is both behavior-unchanged AND its source is byte-identical to the pre-lever baseline. AST parse OK, py_compile OK, and the 82 passing tests import the module.\n\nOUT OF SCOPE / LEFT ALONE (intentionally): (1) topic-driven structure win 1cf3308 untouched — verified not in the diff. (2) scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:153 still does os.environ.setdefault('PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE','1'); that is the TREATMENT compose script for the refuted lever and now sets a dead env var (nothing reads it). Task A scoped removal to multi_section_generator + the 8 tests, so I left that script untouched — it is harmless but you may want to retire the whole s3gear329 treatment script separately. The independent _NUMERIC_TOKEN_RE definitions in access_bypass.py and overstatement_guard.py are unrelated module-local regexes and were correctly not touched.\n\nDeepTRACE SCORING (mission CONTEXT, not covered by this StructuredOutput schema): I did NOT run the DeepTRACE head-to-head in this task — the schema I was given is scoped strictly to the Task-A bank cleanup (removed/tests_pass/control_unchanged/commit/notes). The DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness scoring + competitor-auditability finding is a separate deliverable and should be dispatched as its own step; flagging so the honest-numbers requirement there is not silently dropped.\n\nBranch bot/outline-agent-box, worktree clean, HEAD fce91ef. Not pushed (no push requested)."
}
DEEPTRACE: {
  "polaris_scored": true,
  "polaris_metrics": "REAL, kimi-k2.6-judged DeepTRACE score for the 0.4447 report (outputs/step3_control/report.md), under the official DeepTRACE 10-source external-harness cap (pack_deeptrace: 10 listed sources selected by greedy set-cover, renumbered S1..S10; sources fetched full-text via firecrawl; 52 relevant statements x 10 sources = 520 support cells judged by moonshotai/kimi-k2.6; 0/520 429s, 0 errors, all cells decided). Report shape: 108 statements, 52 relevant (cited), 10 listed sources, sum_C=55 citation links.\n I  One-Sided:            N/A (null) — general query, not a debate (is_debate=False)\n II Overconfident:        N/A (null) — general query\n III Relevant-Statements: 0.4815 (52/108, higher-better)\n IV Uncited-Sources:      0.0000 (all 10 listed sources are cited; lower-better)\n V  Unsupported-Stmts:    0.3269 (17/52 relevant stmts had no supporter among the 10 capped sources; lower-better)\n VI Source-Necessity:     1.0000 (min set-cover = 10/10; n_sole_supporter=10; higher-better)\n VII Citation-Accuracy:   0.5818 (sum(C&S)/sum(C); higher-better)\n VIII Citation-Thoroughness: 0.6154 (sum(C&S)/sum(S); higher-better)\nHONEST CAVEATS: (1) self_rescore re-implementation, kimi-k2.6 substituted for the paper's GPT-5 (disclosed in the lock) — an estimate, not a paper-comparable absolute. (2) The 10-source cap deflates Unsupported/Accuracy/Thoroughness: the packer drops citations to the other 27 of 37 bibliography sources, so a claim whose true supporter was a non-top-10 source reads as unsupported here — the moderate 0.33 unsupported / 0.58 accuracy are a conservative cap artifact, not fabrication. Every one of the 52 relevant claims WAS judgeable against a fetched source (that is the auditability point).",
  "competitors_scoreable": "no",
  "competitor_finding": "NONE of the 4 competitor reports can be scored on DeepTRACE — the citation matrix C[i][j] (statement i cites source j) is unconstructable because none pair parseable inline [N] markers with a resolving numbered bibliography. Verified with the DeepTRACE packer's own parser (pack_deeptrace): all 4 yield an EMPTY citation matrix (0 [N] markers resolvable to any [N]->URL bibliography entry). Counts:\n - ChatGPT_Scoped.md (150K chars, 2007 split stmts): 117 OPAQUE ChatGPT UI chip tokens (private-use-unicode-wrapped 'citeturnNNviewM' navigation chips, resolvable only inside the ChatGPT UI, mapping to NO URL in the export), only 9 stray [N] markers — and those are fragments of QUOTED external bibliographies (source-nums 2,21,43,55,104,2023), 0 of which resolve to a document bibliography. 0 numbered [N]->URL entries. UN-AUDITABLE.\n - ChatGPT_Unscoped.md: 180 opaque UI chip tokens, 0 [N], 0 URLs, 0 bibliography. Pure prose, no resolvable sources at all. UN-AUDITABLE.\n - Gemini_Scoped.md (551K chars, 674 stmts): 0 inline [N] markers; has an END source-LIST (32 numbered 'N. title, accessed..., [url](url)' entries, 46 distinct URLs) but NO per-claim citation binding in the body — you cannot tell which claim any source supports. UN-AUDITABLE claim-by-claim.\n - Gemini_Unscoped.md (334K chars, 339 stmts): 0 inline [N] markers; 37-entry end source-list (48 URLs), no claim->source binding. UN-AUDITABLE claim-by-claim.\nBy contrast POLARIS report.md has 55 parseable [N] links across 52 statements, a 37-entry [N] Title -- URL (tier) bibliography that every marker resolves to, and fetchable source content — the ONLY one of the 5 with a constructable citation matrix.",
  "preflight_pass": true,
  "head_to_head": "No head-to-head DeepTRACE number is possible, and I did NOT fabricate one. Only POLARIS is scoreable: its 8-metric kimi-k2.6-judged card is above (520/520 support cells decided, 0 429). The 4 competitors are structurally un-scoreable on DeepTRACE (empty citation matrix — see competitor_finding), so there is no competitor column to compare against. The honest result is an AUDITABILITY asymmetry, not a score gap: POLARIS is claim-by-claim auditable (every relevant claim maps to a [N] source that was fetched and judged for support), while ChatGPT emits opaque UI citation chips (117/180 tokens that resolve to nothing outside its UI) and Gemini provides only an unbound end-of-report source list with zero inline claim->source markers. Self_rescore comparability requires scoring every competitor with the SAME kimi judge on the SAME task; that is impossible here because the competitors expose no (statement, source) support cells to judge. POLARIS wins the faithfulness axis by being the only verifiable report, not by a manufactured number.",
  "cost_note": "Preflight: PASS (moonshotai/kimi-k2.6, lock signed, 20 operational OpenRouter providers vs floor 8, comparability=self_rescore). Judge spend: 520 kimi-k2.6 support calls (6-cell pilot + 514) = $1.85 total (pilot $0.0235 + full $1.8273), 0/520 HTTP 429, 0 errors — support matrix COMPLETE, not partial, so the number is claimable under the lock. Source fetching: 10/10 sources fetched full-text via firecrawl (jina returned 403 even with JINA_API_KEY; firecrawl handled both HTML and PDFs). Note: judged 520 cells (52 relevant stmts x 10 capped sources), higher than the lock's ~178 estimate because POLARIS's fine-grained deterministic statement split produces more cited units than the paper's GPT core-statement decomposition — more calls, more conservative, disclosed. Artifacts: /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json (metrics+full S/C matrices), /tmp/dt_sources/judge.py (driver), /tmp/dt_sources/S1..S10.txt (fetched sources), /tmp/dt_sources/record.json (packed DeepTRACE record). API key never printed."
}
Answer bluntly: (1) What are POLARIS's real DeepTRACE faithfulness numbers, and are they strong
(auditable, high citation-accuracy/thoroughness, low unsupported)? (2) Can we make a genuine head-to-head
claim vs ChatGPT/Gemini on faithfulness, or are they un-auditable — and if un-auditable, is THAT the
honest moat (POLARIS can be verified claim-by-claim; frontier products cannot)? (3) The honest one-line
positioning: on RACE we're competitive-but-length-capped at 0.44; on faithfulness we are ___. (4) Is
the 'we beat everyone on the axis that matters' claim TRUE and defensible, or is it an incomparable we
should state as such? Do not flatter — the operator wants the truth, not a trophy.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

print(json.dumps(m,indent=1)[:1500])
" 2>&1 | head -50; ls /tmp/dt_sources/ 2>&1

content.text:
The artifact checks out — /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json contains exactly the reported metrics (520 cells judged, 0 errors, kimi-k2.6, $1.83), and all four competitor files exist as described. Verdict follows.

**(1) POLARIS's real DeepTRACE numbers — are they strong?**
Real, complete, kimi-k2.6-judged (520/520 support cells, 0 429s), from /tmp/dt_sources/result_full.json:

- Relevant-Statements: 0.4815 (52/108 cited)
- Uncited-Sources: 0.0000 (perfect — every listed source is used)
- Unsupported-Statements: 0.3269 (17/52)
- Source-Necessity: 1.0000 (perfect — every source in the min cover)
- Citation-Accuracy: 0.5818
- Citation-Thoroughness: 0.6154
- One-Sided / Overconfident: N/A (general query)

Blunt read: **auditable, yes — uniformly strong, no.** Uncited-Sources 0.0 and Source-Necessity 1.0 are genuinely excellent (no citation padding, no dead sources). But 0.33 unsupported and 0.58 accuracy are *moderate*, not elite. The 10-source cap is a real, disclosed deflator (citations to the other 27 of 37 bibliography sources are dropped, so their claims read as unsupported), but you cannot claim the uncapped numbers would be great — you didn't measure them. Also: kimi-k2.6 substitutes for the paper's judge, so these are self-rescore estimates, not paper-comparable absolutes. Say all of this whenever you use the numbers.

**(2) Genuine head-to-head vs ChatGPT/Gemini?**
**No head-to-head number exists, and none can be honestly manufactured.** All 4 competitor reports yield an empty citation matrix under the DeepTRACE packer's own parser: ChatGPT exports opaque private-use-unicode UI chips (117 and 180 tokens) that resolve to no URL outside its UI; Gemini has end-of-report source lists but zero inline claim→source bindings. You cannot judge (statement, source) support cells that don't exist. And yes — **the un-auditability IS the honest finding**, with one discipline: un-auditable ≠ unfaithful. You may not imply the competitors' claims are unsupported; you may only say they are *unverifiable claim-by-claim in their exported form*, while every one of POLARIS's 52 cited claims was traced to a fetched source and judged. That's a real, defensible moat (provenance/auditability), especially for regulated or scientific users. It is a structural property of the products' exports, not a quality score.

**(3) One-line positioning:**
"On RACE we are competitive-but-length-capped at 0.4447; on faithfulness we are **the only one of the five reports that is claim-by-claim auditable — every cited claim independently traced to a fetched source and machine-verified (0% uncited sources, 100% source necessity) — while ChatGPT and Gemini exports cannot be audited at all.**"

**(4) Is 'we beat everyone on the axis that matters' TRUE?**
**No — as a score claim, it is false and you must not say it.** There are no competitor scores to beat; "beat" implies a comparison that structurally cannot be run. This is an *incomparable*, and the honest framing is exactly that: POLARIS **passes an audit the competitors cannot even take**. The defensible claims are: (a) POLARIS is the only report with a constructable citation matrix; (b) it scores perfectly on citation hygiene (uncited-sources 0.0, necessity 1.0) and moderately on capped support metrics with disclosed cap artifact; (c) frontier product exports are un-auditable claim-by-claim. Claim the auditability asymmetry loudly; claim a head-to-head victory never. Also don't oversell "the axis that matters" — 0.58 capped citation-accuracy means roughly 4 in 10 capped citation links weren't judged supportive; a hostile reviewer will find that number, so lead with it yourself.

**Bottom line:** Real numbers, honest caveats, no fabricated comparison. The truthful trophy is "verifiable vs. unverifiable," not "winner vs. losers" — and the former is actually the stronger, harder-to-attack claim.
